mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates broad economic strength and corporate health, leading to upward revisions in earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:Payroll growth is a primary indicator of economic health watched by the Fed and investors.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between PAYEMS 3-month change and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is not statistically greater than zero over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates past a critical threshold, it triggers expectations of hawkish central bank policy, increasing discount rates and causing a negative repricing of equities over the medium term.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) and restrictive monetary policy (high FEDFUNDS) signals a late-cycle economy at risk of overheating, where the central bank is actively tapping the brakes, creating a negative environment for equities.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return when both conditions are met is not statistically significantly lower than when only one or neither condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary environment, accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) signal a robust recovery in consumer spending, which has a high beta to corporate revenues and drives strong positive equity returns as confidence rebuilds.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes approximately 2/3 of US GDP.
failure_condition: The signal shows no positive information coefficient when tested exclusively on data points within 24 months following an NBER-declared recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) serves as a leading indicator of a broad economic slowdown, signaling falling corporate profits and causing negative forward equity returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a key component of the Conference Board Leading Economic Index.
failure_condition: The mean 60-day return in triggered periods is not statistically lower than in non-triggered periods (t-test p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in housing starts (HOUST) reflects high consumer confidence and positive wealth effects, stimulating broader economic activity through construction and related sectors, leading to positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 12-month change in HOUST and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative or statistically insignificant over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A surprisingly strong real GDP growth print confirms robust economic fundamentals, causing analysts to revise earnings forecasts upwards and boosting investor sentiment, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The average 5-day return following a GDP release meeting the trigger is not statistically greater than the unconditional average 5-day return.

mechanism_narrative: When accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) co-occurs with a strong labor market (PAYEMS growth), it signals that demand is outstripping supply, forcing the Federal Reserve to pursue a more aggressive tightening path, which is negative for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Reflects the Fed's dual mandate in a late-cycle context.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 for 20 days upon a trigger fails to produce a negative Sharpe ratio over a 10-year period.
